page: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated CPU virtual server powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The server is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.91 GB per core, and includes 160 GB of local SSD storage. No GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and bzip3 single-threaded decompression, alongside average single-core Geekbench scores. However, multi-core performance is comparatively weak, as evidenced by lower multi-core scores in stress-ng, Geekbench, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is also a limiting factor, with read-only bandwidth categorized as weak, though write bandwidth for large blocks remains average.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ccx23 offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, and light web serving. However, it is less optimal for highly parallelized workloads, software compilation, database operations, or medium-to-large language model inference, where benchmarks indicate weaker performance. This server represents a dedicated-resource entry point, balancing dedicated CPU guarantees with moderate memory capacity.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated CPU virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores with hyperthreading), 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage. The server does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, though multi-core execution, memory read bandwidth, and database operations show weaker results. Qualitatively, the ccx23 provides a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of larger instances. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, single-threaded processing, text processing, and light web serving, while being less optimal for highly parallelized tasks or intensive database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server offering balanced memory and local SSD storage for single-threaded and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC Milan CPU with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
16.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Weak multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth limitations
Best suited for general-purpose applications and text processing tasks
